articles.title: X relaunches a rebuilt Android app after year-long effort
articles.summary: X has relaunched its Android app after nearly a year of rebuilding it from scratch to improve loading, scrolling, notifications, reliability, and future feature development. The effort was described as one of the largest engineering projects in the company's history and is aimed partly at strengthening X in Android-heavy global markets. Some features are still unfinished, including better support for older devices, Spaces, video editing, react-with-video, cashtags, and custom timelines.

articles.title: Apple Briefly Became the World's Most Valuable Company, Toppling Nvidia
articles.summary: Apple briefly overtook Nvidia as the world's most valuable company after investors sold off AI-related semiconductor stocks, reflecting growing skepticism about the long-term returns of massive AI infrastructure spending. Unlike many of its rivals, Apple has spent relatively little on building AI infrastructure and instead benefits from its installed base of 2.5 billion devices. As a result, it is increasingly seen as well positioned to monetize AI features, especially after the positive reception to its revamped Siri.

articles.title: Agentio's AI-powered Creator Ads are Coming to Facebook and Instagram
articles.summary: Agentio, an AI-powered creator advertising platform previously focused on YouTube, has partnered with Meta to bring its tools to Facebook and Instagram. Brands can now access AI-driven insights, creator matching, and partnership ads across both platforms, with the option to run cross-platform campaigns spanning Meta and Google properties. Beta testing showed Agentio's Meta partnership ads achieved 81% higher ROAS and 89% higher click-through rates compared to brands' non-Agentio creator ads.

articles.title: Product Quality: A Shared Commitment to Craft in the Wake of AI
articles.summary: As AI collapses the gap between "nothing" and "adequate," genuine craft becomes the only remaining differentiator between products. Craft is a shared discipline across Utility, Usability, and Feel—owned collectively by engineers, PMs, designers, and leaders, not design alone. "Would I recommend this to someone I care about?" should be the standard for judging whether a product has truly earned people's affection, not just their approval.

articles.title: Accessibility Isn't a Checklist. It's a Design Decision You're Avoiding
articles.summary: A UX designer recounts watching a blind user struggle through an 11-minute flight booking process that should have taken 90 seconds, revealing how years of seemingly reasonable decisions had quietly excluded users with disabilities. The experience showed that accessibility isn't primarily a technical challenge or a compliance checklist—it requires making it a core product priority, testing with real assistive technology users, and treating accessibility as part of the definition of "done" rather than something to address later.

articles.title: Why the Future of Design Must Begin with Understanding People
articles.summary: User experience has always extended beyond visible interfaces, shaped instead by trust, psychology, and human context. Drawing on architecture, emergency medical care, and design experience, the author contends AI heightens designers' responsibility to build informed—not blind—trust, making AI literacy an essential design skill. Design's future depends on understanding people rather than mastering tools, since technology changes but human needs remain constant.

articles.title: Remix High-performing Ads for Your Brand (Website)
articles.summary: Browse a wall of proven ad creatives from top brands, drop in your brand, and get an on-brand video or static ad in minutes.

articles.title: Datatype (Website)
articles.summary: Datatype is an OpenType variable font that turns simple text expressions into inline charts. No JavaScript, no images, and no rendering library.

articles.title: AI Skills for Product Designers (Website)
articles.summary: Whether you're directing your AI or working alongside it, Layers walks you through all seven layers of product design, so the decisions underneath the screen actually get made.

articles.title: The “mocked, then loved” AirPods effect
articles.summary: Apple's AirPods and Apple Watch both overcame early ridicule by proving genuinely useful in everyday life, but the rumored camera-equipped AirPods face a different challenge: concerns about privacy and social acceptance rather than just unconventional design. While Apple has a strong track record of turning skepticism into success, the new AirPods will need to demonstrate clear, hands-free AI benefits while convincing both wearers and those around them that the cameras can be trusted.

articles.title: Against Design System Federation
articles.summary: Federated design system governance—used by just 13% of teams—is losing favor due to poor contribution rates, diffused accountability, and inflated cost-saving expectations. Layoffs and AI-driven productivity narratives have fueled interest in federation, but it typically damages system quality, user experience, and team morale rather than fixing organizational problems. Federation works best as one aspect within a hybrid model, not as a standalone governance approach, since it demands more organizational maturity than most companies achieve.

articles.title: Ask yourself where you can add value
articles.summary: Designers should stop undervaluing in-house work and instead showcase the business problems they solved, the constraints they worked within, and the measurable results they achieved. Rather than waiting to build a perfect portfolio, improve existing projects incrementally with stronger storytelling, updated visuals, and small enhancements, making steady progress in short bursts that fit around a busy schedule.

articles.title: How AI Helped Us Make Sense of Hundreds of Components
articles.summary: A UK higher education institution had years of organically grown web components with no clear inventory, so a team built an AI-assisted audit tool through rapid, conversational iteration rather than a fixed spec.

articles.title: This Mac app can turn every window into a CRT fever dream or a nostalgic Game Boy
articles.summary: Glaze is a Mac app that transforms your entire display with over 50 real-time visual effects—from CRT monitors to VHS tapes—while also offering productivity and accessibility modes.
